sourceMovie.title: The Untouchables
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Untouchables works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Public Enemies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Public Enemies shares The Untouchables's gift for crime — the 2009 film delivers history without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Road to Perdition
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Untouchables compelling — the crime, the thriller — shows up in Road to Perdition (2002) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Widows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of The Untouchables is alive in Widows (2018), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Layer Cake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Layer Cake (2004) operates in the same crime as The Untouchables, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Highwaymen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Highwaymen (2019) belongs to the same conversation as The Untouchables — both anchor their crime in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Miller's Crossing
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Untouchables's crime stayed with you, Miller's Crossing (1990) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Outfit
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Untouchables excels in crime, The Outfit (2022) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Copshop
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Copshop (2021) is the natural next step after The Untouchables: same crime DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Thief
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Untouchables who respond to its crime will find Thief (1981) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: No Sudden Move
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Untouchables fans keep landing on No Sudden Move (2021) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Untouchables?
faq.answer: Try Public Enemies, Road to Perdition, Widows—each shares DNA with The Untouchables while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Untouchables?
faq.answer: The Untouchables (1987) is best described as Crime, History, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Untouchables worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
